---
title: Shrinkage
jupyter: python3
---

### Set up and load data

```{python}

## Import modules
import abct
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from scipy import io

## Load data
data = io.loadmat("./data/hcp_data.mat")

```

### Visualize structural networks

```{python}

## Get structural networks
WD = dict()
WD["Original"] = data["W"]
WD["Proximity"] = data["D"]**(-1)
np.fill_diagonal(WD["Proximity"], 0)
WD["Shrunken proximity"] = abct.shrinkage(WD["Proximity"])

ordw = data["ordw"].flatten() - 1
zmin = (0, 0, 0)
zmax = (0.01, 0.01, 0.01)
for i, name in enumerate(WD):
    fig = px.imshow(
        WD[name][np.ix_(ordw, ordw)],
        color_continuous_scale="inferno",
        zmin=zmin[i],
        zmax=zmax[i],
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"{name} structural network",
        title_x=0.5,
        width=600,
        height=600,
    )
    fig.show()

```

### Scatter plots of connectivity and proximity networks

```{python}

neye = ~np.eye(len(data["W"]), dtype=bool) 

# Scatter plot of structural and proximity networks
r = np.corrcoef(WD["Original"][neye], WD["Proximity"][neye])[0, 1]
fig = px.scatter(x=WD["Original"][neye], y=WD["Proximity"][neye])
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Network weights of structural network",
    yaxis_title="Network weights of proximity network",
    title=f"Structural and proximity networks (r = {r:.3f})",
    title_x=0.5,
    width=600,
    height=600,
)
fig.show()

# Scatter plot of structural and shrunken proximity networks
r = np.corrcoef(WD["Original"][neye], WD["Shrunken proximity"][neye])[0, 1]
fig = px.scatter(x=WD["Original"][neye], y=WD["Shrunken proximity"][neye])
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Network weights of structural network",
    yaxis_title="Network weights of shrunken proximity network",
    title=f"Structural and shrunken proximity networks (r = {r:.3f})",
    title_x=0.5,
    width=600,
    height=600,
)
fig.show()

```
